_notes: Seed data — see RESEARCH_SPEC_PHONE_POLICIES.md. Many entries are confidence:low and need verification. Districts not present default to tier 1 (no policy) in the UI.

2502790.policySummary: Yondr pouch program available across roughly 31 BPS schools (4+ high schools, 9 elementary) on opt-in basis under a 3-year contract. Not yet a uniform district-wide K-12 mandate.

sources.title: Some high schoolers say a school cell phone ban boosted grades and connections
Phone Restrictions Make 'Immense Difference' at Boston Schools

2504830.policySummary: District-wide Yondr pouch ban: all electronic devices secured in locked Yondr pouches upon entry, returned at end of day.

sources.title: Mass. AG cell phone policy recommendations

2507740.policySummary: Cited by state Senate sponsors as an early adopter of a full school-day phone ban. Verify hardware-vs-honor enforcement specifics.

sources.title: Mass. lawmakers approve cell phone ban for public schools

2508610.policySummary: District has adopted a phone ban; specific enforcement mechanism (hardware vs. off-and-away) not yet verified at K-12 scope.

sources.title: Mass. Senate passes school cell phone ban

2503150.policySummary: Phone storage measures (off-and-away style) reported in statewide coverage; not full hardware-level ban district-wide.

2512660.policySummary: Yondr pouches in use at the middle schools since 2024; high schools not yet on the same hardware regimen.
